answers: "{\"delivery_process\":\"We deliver with our own driver/ will call/ UPS and FedEx. So many jobs get to shipping the same day they need to be delivered.\",\"project_management\":\"We use Pace as our MIS system. It has its limitations with clear information.\",\"team_size\":\"We have a team of up to 25 people in production. All working in different departments with some able to float and do other things in different departments.\",\"bottlenecks\":\"Every department can have a bottleneck depending on the workload. The one pinch point would be the cutting department consistently working ahead.\",\"quality_metrics\":\"We go off a spreadsheet daily for jobs that are due that day. Due dates are how we prioritize the jobs that we're working on.\",\"client_feedback\":\"Quality of the work and missing due dates.\"}"

"{\"current_systems\":\"Microsoft 365, EPACE MIS, LOOM\",\"tech_stack\":\"Microsoft 365, EPACE MIS, LOOM, Planned Prophet, xmpie, storeflow/ustore, adobe suite\",\"automation_level\":\"basic\",\"pain_points\":\"Auditing (orders, storeflow, estimates, processes) and Retraining thats personal but department wise Prepress\",\"data_quality\":\"7/10\",\"integration_challenges\":\"a Programming capability to automate how we do prepress is our biggest time suck at the moment, it also causes the most mistakes and rework due to human error. We have too many manual touch points in that department and our software does not communicate or automate hardly any of it.\"}"

"{\"strategic_focus\":\"Strengthen operational infrastructure so the company can scale without breaking process.  Increase profitability through tighter financial controls (AR discipline, job costing accuracy, AP audits). Continue to standardize systems so data lives in one place and decisions are measurable.  Focus on sales structures that support long term relationship growth not short term transactional wins.  Continue to reduce dependency on tribal knowledge and move toward documented, repeatable SOP-driven operations.\",\"operational_bottlenecks\":\"Our primary bottleneck is operational scalability. While the company has strong institutional knowledge and experienced team members, a significant amount of process ownership and decision-making remains concentrated within a small group. This creates capacity constraints and limits speed of execution.  Additionally, many of our administrative and internal workflows are still relatively manual and “hand-held.” Although we have begun implementing system driven tools (queues), much of our day-to-day execution still depends on individual follow-up, email communication, and legacy habits rather than standardized, automated processes.\\nWe are in transition progress has started but we have not yet reached a fully structured, scalable operating model. Strengthening documentation, accountability, and system integration will be critical to supporting future growth.\",\"cross_departmental_friction\":\"We continue to experience classic “us vs. them” dynamics between Sales, Administration, and Production. While collaboration exists, alignment is inconsistent.  A recurring pattern is that inefficiencies in upfront processes estimating clarity, job intake accuracy, scope changes, deposit timing, and administrative delays compress production timelines. Production is then expected to absorb that lost time while still meeting delivery commitments. This creates avoidable stress, reactive decision-making, and strain between departments. We have begun building better visibility through system queues, but we have not yet fully aligned expectations, ownership, and process discipline across departments. Greater standardization at intake and clearer accountability at each stage would significantly reduce friction.\",\"transformation_appetite\":\"1-3\",\"resource_allocation\":\"Higher compensated team members spend time on data cleanup, status chasing, manual follow-ups, or rework. Production time is reallocated to rush jobs caused by upstream delays or late approvals. This disrupts planned workflow and reduces overall equipment efficiency. We can generate substantial operational data (job costing, turnaround times, rework), but it is not consistently used.\\nJobs pass through multiple hands for clarifications, corrections, or missing information. Each additional touch increases labor cost and the risk of error. Poor batching increase make-ready time and reduce press efficiency. Strategic leadership time is consumed by day to day problem solving rather than growth strategy, or process optimization.\",\"growth_constraints\":\"We remain too dependent on individual knowledge, manual oversight, and reactive problem-solving. While we have strong team members and solid revenue, our processes are not yet sufficiently standardized or system-driven to support efficient scalability. The TechnaPrint acquisition is a clear example of broader structural constraints. Post-acquisition integration, relationship transition, and proactive retention efforts were not as structured or deliberate as they needed to be. Growth requires defined ownership, clear transition plans, and early customer engagement strategies not just operational absorption.\\n\\nSimilarly, when we consolidated two additional locations into our primary facility, we did not establish a sufficiently detailed integration plan outlining expectations, roles, and process alignment across all shops. As a result, integration relied more on adaptation over time than on a clearly designed framework.\"}"

"{\"top_priorities\":\"Rebranding\\nEquipment Purchases\\nEvaluate Acquisition\",\"biggest_frustration\":\"Market & price pressure\\naging workforce\\nweak sales team\",\"ideal_state\":\"more automation\\nless rework\\nhappier staff\",\"workflow_stuck\":\"prepress - takes too long to get proofs out, systems aren't as automated as they should be and we employ too many people in this department according to standard industry ratios\",\"team_bottlenecks\":\"I am stretched too thin and we have a fairly flat org chart\",\"owner_bottlenecks\":\"way too many\",\"systems_struggles\":\"we use email, we use a CRM, we use Pace (MIS), we have prepress software, we use excel and chat gpt\",\"communication_breakdown\":\"we could be better at communicating internally\",\"manual_firefighting\":\"prepress imposition is too manual and proofing is still too manual\",\"growth_barriers\":\"decline in traditional print and aging workforce plus weak sales team.  They take orders but they do not provide a ton of value.  \",\"volume_breaking_point\":\"our customer service team is too manual.\",\"ai_opportunities\":\"order input, accounting, creating tickets and processing data in mailing and prepress \",\"if_nothing_else_changes_but_X_this_was_worth_it\":\"communicating with respect even when we disagree\",\"role_label\":\"Owner\",\"department_key\":\"owner\",\"display_name\":\"Melissa Koke\",\"preferred_reference\":\"Melissa Koke\",\"top_3_issues\":[\"aging workforce unable or unwilling to use technology to simplify what we do\",\"\\\"this is how we've always done it\\\" mentality\",\"communicating with respect even when people disagree\"],\"primary_kpis\":[\"net profit\",\"sales per employee\",\"low rework\"],\"kpi_baselines\":{\"net profit\":\"last year - 2%\",\"sales per employee\":\"currently about $150K, too low\",\"low rework\":\"currently developing this system\"},\"change_readiness\":\"medium\",\"weekly_capacity_for_implementation_hours\":0,\"biggest_risk_if_we_push_too_fast\":\"Buy in from staff\"}"
